protocol.name: zcosmic_tooltip_v1
protocol.copyright:

protocol.description:
This protocol allows clients to create tooltip surfaces that the compositor positions relative to the pointer cursor automatically. Instead of the client receiving pointer motion events and issuing xdg_popup.reposition() round-trips, the compositor tracks the pointer position internally and repositions the tooltip surface each frame, eliminating latency.
The client provides a tooltip surface and a parent surface. While the pointer is over the parent surface, the compositor positions the tooltip at pointer_position + offset. The client configures the offset and anchor via requests on the tooltip object.

interface.name: zcosmic_tooltip_manager_v1
interface.description: Manager for creating compositor-driven tooltip objects.

request.name: destroy
request.description: Informs the server that the client will no longer use this global. Existing tooltip objects remain valid until destroyed.

request.name: get_tooltip
request.description:
Create a compositor-driven tooltip controller for the given surface.
The tooltip_surface is the wl_surface that will be displayed as the tooltip. The parent_surface is the wl_surface that the tooltip is associated with — the compositor will position the tooltip relative to the pointer only while the pointer is over the parent surface.
If the given tooltip_surface already has a zcosmic_tooltip_v1 object associated, the already_exists protocol error is raised.

entry.name: already_exists
entry.summary: tooltip_surface already has a tooltip object

interface.name: zcosmic_tooltip_v1
interface.description:
Controls a compositor-driven tooltip surface. The compositor positions the tooltip surface at the pointer position plus the configured offset, constrained to the output boundaries.
The tooltip is shown when the pointer enters the parent surface and hidden when the pointer leaves. The client must still commit content to the tooltip surface — the compositor only handles positioning.
All position values are in surface-local coordinates of the parent.

request.name: destroy
request.description: Destroys the tooltip controller. The tooltip surface will be hidden immediately.

request.name: set_offset
request.description:
Set the offset in logical pixels from the pointer position to the tooltip surface's top-left corner. This is double-buffered and applied on the next wl_surface.commit of the tooltip surface.
Default offset is (0, 0).

request.name: set_anchor
request.description:
Set which corner of the tooltip surface is anchored to the pointer position (plus offset). This is double-buffered and applied on the next wl_surface.commit of the tooltip surface.
Default anchor is top_left.

request.name: set_show_delay

description.summary: set delay before showing the tooltip

request.description:
Set the delay in milliseconds before the tooltip is shown after the pointer enters the parent surface. If set to 0 (the default), the tooltip is shown immediately when the pointer enters the parent.
When a non-zero delay is set, the tooltip remains hidden until the pointer has been over the parent surface continuously for the specified duration. If the pointer leaves the parent surface before the delay expires, the timer resets.
When a delay is set, the tooltip is positioned at the pointer location at the moment the delay expires. The tooltip stays at that fixed position (it does not follow the pointer) until it is hidden when the pointer leaves the parent surface.
This is double-buffered and applied on the next wl_surface.commit of the tooltip surface.

enum.name: anchor
enum.description: Which corner of the tooltip is placed at pointer + offset.

event.name: reposition
event.description:
Sent by the compositor when the tooltip surface has been repositioned. The x and y values are the position in the parent surface's coordinate space. This event is sent each time the compositor updates the tooltip position (i.e. on each pointer motion frame).
The client may use this for informational purposes but does not need to respond — the compositor handles all positioning.

entry.name: surface_destroyed
entry.summary: the associated surface has been destroyed
